Apple___Apple_scab.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis. Causes olive-green to black spots on leaves and fruit, leading to premature leaf drop and fruit deformation.
Apple___Apple_scab.recommendation: Apply fungicides during bud break and after petal fall. Remove fallen leaves and fruit. Prune for better air circulation. Plant resistant varieties.
Apple___Apple_scab.preventionTips: Choose scab-resistant apple varieties. Maintain proper spacing between trees. Avoid overhead irrigation. Apply preventive fungicides in early spring.

Apple___Black_rot.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Botryosphaeria obtusa. Causes purple leaf spots that turn brown with black margins, and black rot on fruit.
Apple___Black_rot.recommendation: Remove mummified fruit and cankers. Apply fungicides during bloom and after petal fall. Prune infected branches.
Apple___Black_rot.preventionTips: Sanitize orchard by removing diseased wood and fruit. Apply dormant sprays. Ensure good air circulation through proper pruning.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.explanation: Fungal disease requiring both cedar and apple trees. Causes orange-yellow spots on leaves and fruit, leading to premature defoliation.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.recommendation: Remove nearby cedar trees if possible. Apply fungicides before bloom and during infection periods. Plant resistant varieties.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.preventionTips: Avoid planting apples near cedar trees. Use rust-resistant apple varieties. Apply preventive fungicides in spring.

Apple___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant appears healthy with normal leaf color and growth.
Apple___healthy.recommendation: Continue regular maintenance including proper watering, fertilization, and pruning. Monitor for any changes.
Apple___healthy.preventionTips: Maintain good orchard hygiene. Regular inspection for early disease detection. Proper nutrition and irrigation.

Blueberry___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color.
Blueberry___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard care including proper soil pH maintenance (4.5-5.5), regular watering, and mulching.
Blueberry___healthy.preventionTips: Maintain acidic soil conditions. Ensure good drainage. Regular pruning for air circulation.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.explanation: Fungal disease causing white powdery coating on leaves, shoots, and fruit. Can lead to reduced fruit quality and yield.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.recommendation: Apply sulfur-based fungicides. Improve air circulation through pruning. Remove infected plant material.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.preventionTips: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain proper plant spacing. Apply preventive fungicides.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth and normal leaf appearance.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.recommendation: Continue regular care including proper pruning, watering, and fertilization. Monitor for pest activity.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.preventionTips: Regular sanitation around trees. Proper pruning techniques. Monitor for common cherry pests.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis. Causes rectangular gray lesions on leaves, reducing photosynthesis and yield.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.recommendation: Apply fungicides at early disease onset. Use resistant hybrids. Rotate crops to reduce inoculum.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.preventionTips: Plant resistant corn hybrids. Practice crop rotation. Avoid continuous corn planting. Use tillage to bury residue.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Puccinia sorghi. Produces reddish-brown pustules on leaves, reducing photosynthetic area.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.recommendation: Apply fungicides when rust is detected early. Plant resistant hybrids. Avoid late planting.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.preventionTips: Use rust-resistant hybrids. Plant early to avoid peak rust season. Scout fields regularly for early detection.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum. Causes long, cigar-shaped lesions on leaves, significantly reducing yield.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.recommendation: Apply fungicides at early disease stages. Use resistant hybrids. Rotate crops and till residue.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.preventionTips: Plant resistant hybrids. Practice crop rotation. Use tillage to bury infected residue. Avoid continuous corn.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and development.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard corn management including proper fertilization, irrigation, and pest monitoring.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.preventionTips: Maintain proper plant density. Ensure adequate nutrition. Regular field scouting.

Grape___Black_rot.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Guignardia bidwellii. Causes brown circular lesions on leaves and fruit rot with black pycnidia.
Grape___Black_rot.recommendation: Apply fungicides from bloom through harvest. Remove mummified fruit. Prune for air circulation.
Grape___Black_rot.preventionTips: Sanitize vineyard by removing diseased fruit. Use resistant varieties when possible. Improve air circulation.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).explanation: Complex fungal disease causing tiger-stripe leaf patterns, fruit bleaching, and vine decline. Difficult to control.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).recommendation: Remove infected wood during pruning. Apply fungicides to pruning wounds. Maintain vine health.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).preventionTips: Avoid large pruning wounds. Disinfect tools between vines. Maintain balanced vine nutrition.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.explanation: Fungal disease causing brown lesions with dark borders on leaves. Can lead to premature defoliation.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.recommendation: Apply fungicides during wet periods. Remove infected leaves. Improve air circulation.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.preventionTips: Manage canopy density. Avoid overhead irrigation. Apply preventive fungicides in wet conditions.

Grape___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Vine shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and fruit development.
Grape___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard vineyard management including proper pruning, irrigation, and nutrition.
Grape___healthy.preventionTips: Regular canopy management. Balanced fertilization. Monitor for pest and disease pressure.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).explanation: Bacterial disease spread by Asian citrus psyllid. Causes yellowing of leaves, bitter fruit, and tree decline. Currently incurable.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).recommendation: Remove infected trees to prevent spread. Control psyllid population. Use healthy nursery stock.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).preventionTips: Monitor for psyllid activity. Use certified disease-free trees. Implement area-wide pest management.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.explanation: Bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas arboricola. Causes small water-soaked spots on leaves, fruit, and twigs.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.recommendation: Apply copper bactericides during dormant season. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove infected tissue.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.preventionTips: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid working with wet foliage. Maintain proper tree spacing.

Peach___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Tree shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and fruit development.
Peach___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard peach care including proper thinning, irrigation, and fertilization.
Peach___healthy.preventionTips: Regular pruning for structure and air circulation. Monitor for common peach diseases.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.explanation: Bacterial disease causing water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit. Can lead to defoliation and fruit loss.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.recommendation: Use disease-free seeds and transplants. Apply copper bactericides. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.preventionTips: Crop rotation. Use resistant varieties. Sanitize equipment. Avoid working with wet plants.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and fruit development.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard pepper care including proper staking, irrigation, and fertilization.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.preventionTips: Maintain proper plant spacing. Monitor for common pepper pests and diseases.

Potato___Early_blight.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. Causes dark concentric rings on lower leaves, leading to premature defoliation.
Potato___Early_blight.recommendation: Apply fungicides at first sign of disease. Remove infected leaves. Ensure proper plant spacing.
Potato___Early_blight.preventionTips: Use resistant varieties. Crop rotation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain plant vigor.

Potato___Late_blight.explanation: Devastating fungal disease caused by Phytophthora infestans. Causes water-soaked lesions that quickly turn black.
Potato___Late_blight.recommendation: Apply protective fungicides before infection. Destroy infected plants immediately. Avoid planting near tomatoes.
Potato___Late_blight.preventionTips: Use resistant varieties. Plant certified seed potatoes. Avoid overhead irrigation. Scout fields regularly.

Potato___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and tuber development.
Potato___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard potato care including proper hilling, irrigation, and pest monitoring.
Potato___healthy.preventionTips: Use certified seed potatoes. Crop rotation. Proper soil drainage.

Raspberry___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Canes show healthy growth with normal leaf color and fruit development.
Raspberry___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard raspberry care including proper pruning, trellising, and irrigation.
Raspberry___healthy.preventionTips: Regular pruning of old canes. Maintain proper spacing. Monitor for common raspberry diseases.

Soybean___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and pod development.
Soybean___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard soybean management including proper planting density, fertilization, and pest monitoring.
Soybean___healthy.preventionTips: Crop rotation. Use disease-free seeds. Regular field scouting.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.explanation: Fungal disease causing white powdery coating on leaves. Reduces photosynthesis and can cause premature death.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.recommendation: Apply sulfur or neem oil fungicides. Remove severely infected leaves. Improve air circulation.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.preventionTips: Plant resistant varieties. Avoid overhead irrigation. Maintain proper plant spacing.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.explanation: Fungal disease causing purple to brown spots on leaves with yellow halos. Can reduce plant vigor and yield.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.recommendation: Remove infected leaves. Apply fungicides during bloom. Use disease-free planting material.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.preventionTips: Use certified disease-free plants. Proper plant spacing. Avoid overhead irrigation.

Strawberry___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and fruit development.
Strawberry___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard strawberry care including proper mulching, irrigation, and renovation.
Strawberry___healthy.preventionTips: Use certified disease-free plants. Proper bed management. Regular renovation.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.explanation: Bacterial disease causing water-soaked spots on leaves, stems, and fruit. Can lead to defoliation and fruit loss.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.recommendation: Use disease-free seeds and transplants. Apply copper bactericides. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.preventionTips: Crop rotation. Use resistant varieties. Sanitize garden tools. Avoid working with wet plants.

Tomato___Early_blight.explanation: Fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani. Causes dark concentric rings on lower leaves, leading to premature defoliation.
Tomato___Early_blight.recommendation: Remove infected lower leaves. Apply copper-based fungicides. Ensure proper spacing for air circulation. Stake plants.
Tomato___Early_blight.preventionTips: Use resistant varieties. Crop rotation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Mulch to prevent soil splash.

Tomato___Late_blight.explanation: Devastating fungal disease caused by Phytophthora infestans. Causes water-soaked lesions that quickly turn black and spread.
Tomato___Late_blight.recommendation: Apply protective fungicides preventatively. Remove infected plants immediately. Avoid planting near potatoes.
Tomato___Late_blight.preventionTips: Use resistant varieties. Plant certified disease-free seedlings. Avoid overhead irrigation. Scout regularly.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.explanation: Fungal disease causing yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces and olive-green mold on undersides. Common in greenhouses.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.recommendation: Improve air circulation and reduce humidity. Apply fungicides. Remove infected leaves.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.preventionTips: Maintain good ventilation in greenhouses. Avoid overhead irrigation. Space plants properly.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.explanation: Fungal disease causing small circular spots with dark borders and gray centers on lower leaves.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.recommendation: Remove infected lower leaves. Apply fungicides. Mulch to prevent soil splash. Stake plants.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.preventionTips: Crop rotation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Remove plant debris. Ensure good air circulation.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.explanation: Pest causing yellow stippling on leaves and fine webbing. Can cause severe defoliation if untreated.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.recommendation: Wash plants with water to remove mites. Apply miticides or insecticidal soap. Encourage natural predators.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.preventionTips: Maintain adequate humidity. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Regularly inspect undersides of leaves.

Tomato___Target_Spot.explanation: Fungal disease causing concentric ring patterns on leaves and fruit. Similar to early blight but with different pathogen.
Tomato___Target_Spot.recommendation: Apply fungicides at first symptoms. Remove infected plant material. Improve air circulation.
Tomato___Target_Spot.preventionTips: Use resistant varieties. Crop rotation. Avoid overhead irrigation. Sanitize garden tools.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.explanation: Viral disease spread by whiteflies. Causes yellowing and curling of leaves, stunted growth, and reduced yield.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.recommendation: Remove infected plants immediately. Control whitefly population. Use virus-resistant varieties.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.preventionTips: Use yellow sticky traps for whiteflies. Plant resistant varieties. Keep area weed-free. Use insect barriers.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.explanation: Viral disease causing mottled light and dark green areas on leaves, stunted growth, and fruit distortion.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.recommendation: Remove infected plants. Disinfect tools. Control aphids and other vectors. Use resistant varieties.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.preventionTips: Use disease-free seeds and transplants. Control aphid populations. Sanitize equipment regularly.

Tomato___healthy.explanation: No visible signs of disease detected. Plant shows healthy growth with normal leaf color and fruit development.
Tomato___healthy.recommendation: Continue standard tomato care including proper staking, pruning, irrigation, and fertilization.
Tomato___healthy.preventionTips: Maintain proper plant spacing. Regular monitoring for pests and diseases. Balanced nutrition.
